XXXV1 of 195\). -- 2 of 3 -- / I ~ ~ ~ .ornrmnrT mrr arro. ~ G. ~o~'(/~ ~G. $ ~~~~ STATE:VU<:XT. Under the existing provisions of the Bombay Court-fees Ad, 1959 (Hom. XXXVI of 1959), all f(JOS chargeable are collected by way of" . impressed and adhesive stamps. puc to spurt in litigations, there is increase in demand of court-fee stamps. Sometimes it is difficult to cater the need of litigants and supply requisite court-fee stamps as per their demand. In the present system, court-fee' is paid by physical cour-t-fee stamp papers. However, there is no mechanism involved to cheek genuineness of these court-fee stamp papers. In the recent past, it is revealed that some people are indulging into systematic: racket of preparing and circulating fake stamp papers resulting into huge loss of revenue. The Government considers it expedient 1,0 devise a system to . overcome these problems. These problems can be resolved by usc of advanced technology, such as introduction of a system providing lor payment through a-payment or payment through Spcured Bank and Treasury Receipt (SBTR) in addition to the prevailing system of payment of court-fees through court-fee stamps. 2. As both Houses of the State Legislature arc not in session and the Governor of Maharashtra is satisfied that circumstances exist which render it necessary for him to take immediate action further to amend the Bombay Court-fees Act, 1959, for the purposes aforesaid, this Ordinance is promulgated. . Mumbai, Dated the 8th June 2011.
